---
title: "Lienar Group"
date: "2026-06-25"
categories: 
  - Math > Abstract Algebra > Group Theory
---

# 1.1. Definition
**(i)** The ***general linear group of degree $n$*** $GL(n, \mathbb{R})$ is the set of all invertible $n \times n$ matrices with real entries. 

$$GL(n, \mathbb{R}) = \{ A \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n} \mid \det(A) \neq 0 \}.$$

**(ii)** The ***special linear group of degree $n$*** $SL(n, \mathbb{R})$ is the set of all invertible $n \times n$ matrices with real entries and determinant 1.

$$SL(n, \mathbb{R}) = \{ A \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n} \mid \det(A) = 1 \}.$$
